body:
This PR gets rid of `GridMetricOperation`, as it seems redundant with `KernelFunctionOperation`. In addition it creates new capabilities to form arithmetic expressions with "coordinates", ie so we can write

```julia
using Oceananigans
grid = RectilinearGrid(size=(4, 4, 4), extent=(4, 4, 4))
c = CenterField(grid)
w = ZFaceField(grid)
set!(c, 1)
using Oceananigans.Operators: z
c_z = Field(c * z)
```

which produces

```
4×4×4 Field{Center, Center, Center} on RectilinearGrid on CPU
├── grid: 4×4×4 RectilinearGrid{Float64, Periodic, Periodic, Bounded} on CPU with 3×3×3 halo
├── boundary conditions: FieldBoundaryConditions
│   └── west: Periodic, east: Periodic, south: Periodic, north: Periodic, bottom: ZeroFlux, top: ZeroFlux, immersed: Nothing
├── operand: BinaryOperation at (Center, Center, Center)
├── status: time=0.0
└── data: 10×10×10 OffsetArray(::Array{Float64, 3}, -2:7, -2:7, -2:7) with eltype Float64 with indices -2:7×-2:7×-2:7
    └── max=-0.5, min=-3.5, mean=-2.0
```

while

```julia
set!(w, 1)
w_z = Field(w * z)
```

produces

```
4×4×5 Field{Center, Center, Face} on RectilinearGrid on CPU
├── grid: 4×4×4 RectilinearGrid{Float64, Periodic, Periodic, Bounded} on CPU with 3×3×3 halo
├── boundary conditions: FieldBoundaryConditions
│   └── west: Periodic, east: Periodic, south: Periodic, north: Periodic, bottom: Nothing, top: Nothing, immersed: Nothing
├── operand: BinaryOperation at (Center, Center, Face)
├── status: time=0.0
└── data: 10×10×11 OffsetArray(::Array{Float64, 3}, -2:7, -2:7, -2:8) with eltype Float64 with indices -2:7×-2:7×-2:8
    └── max=0.0, min=-4.0, mean=-2.0
```

I've made this a draft because I am unsure about a few things. First, it is pretty significant to invoke names like "x", "y", "z", etc, but I think it's warranted in this case. We can keep them hidden inside the `Operators` module? Also I'm not sure it makes sense to get rid of `GridMetricOperation`. But it seems like extra code that isn't necessary. If we want to move forward with this then I will add a simple test.

Finally, I think we should change `xnodes`, `ynodes`, etc to return the `grid_metric_operation` (in the same way as `xspacing`, etc). But I am also not sure why we don't do that now.
title: Eliminate GridMetricOperation and create node metrics

body: This PR adds a test for running a simulation with a DateTime clock, including proper interpolation of a `FieldTimeSeries`-based forcing.
title: Support time stepping with DateTime clock

body:
This reduces the badges from three rows to two rows (deleting a few badges: repo status, docker status, CI status), and also compresses the description into one paragraph.

I'd like to spruce up the README in other ways (and also update the performance benchmarks) but this is a good start.
title: Simplify README header

body:
CUDA.jl v5.9.0 includes https://github.com/JuliaGPU/CUDA.jl/pull/2869 which resolves a bunch of issues here so I think we should set the `[compat]` entry for CUDA.jl to v5.9+.

Technically v5.8.4 also includes the fixes although there's only one commit difference between them so I went for v5.9.

Resolves #4047
Resolves #4694
Resolves #4663

labels.description: Where Oceananigans gets its powers from
labels.name: GPU 👾

title: (0.100.1) Set CUDA.jl compatibility to v5.9+
